objectives: Comprehensive architecture review of mx20022 22-crate workspace
Evaluate crate boundaries, dependency direction, trait abstractions, SOLID compliance, PRD alignment
important_facts: Clean DAG dependency graph - no circular dependencies confirmed via cargo tree
22 crates total: runtime-core, runtime, channels (7 impls), store (3 impls), participants, correlation, session, config, metrics, admin, crypto, cli
Dependency direction is correct: lower-level crates (runtime-core, store, channels) have no upward dependencies
runtime-core depends only on mx20022-metrics - slight concern about metrics coupling in core
Duplicate Outcome enum exists in mx20022-store and mx20022-runtime-core
build_participants in app.rs is a ~200-line match factory - OCP violation
Engine owns all channel construction in a massive match block - another OCP violation
main.rs has 7-way if/else chain for service mode combinations (pipeline/admin-http/admin-grpc)
Session crate is structurally isolated - no consumer in the workspace
CorrelationEngine uses single RwLock HashMap - write contention under load
Store default implementations load all records to count them (count_dead_letters, count_pending_expectations)
TransactionManager has no timeout enforcement - PRD requires timeout_ms per pipeline
No outbound channel integration in the engine - only inbound channels are wired
No Context Space (typed map) for parsed messages - PRD shows ctx.message() returning parsed type
State strings used for store layer instead of enum - stringly-typed boundary
InboundMessage/OutboundMessage are bare String wrappers with no metadata
artifacts: crates/mx20022-runtime/src/ (lib.rs, app.rs, engine.rs, main.rs, domain.rs, application.rs)
risks_issues: CRITICAL: No outbound channel wiring - engine only processes inbound, never sends responses
CRITICAL: No pipeline timeout enforcement despite PRD requirement and config field
CRITICAL: Duplicate Outcome enum creates mapping layer that adds no value
HIGH: build_participants factory violates OCP - every new participant requires modifying app.rs
HIGH: CorrelationEngine single-node only - RwLock HashMap does not scale horizontally
HIGH: Store trait is too broad - 20+ methods mixing transaction, correlation, dead-letter concerns
MEDIUM: Session crate is orphaned - no integration point in any other crate
MEDIUM: main.rs combinatorial explosion for service modes
MEDIUM: No graceful shutdown orchestration across channels/store/correlation

iteration.summary: Completed comprehensive architecture review of the full 22-crate mx20022 payments runtime workspace. Read all key source files, dependency graphs, and the full PRD. Identified clean DAG structure and well-designed core abstractions (Participant trait, Store trait, Channel traits). Found critical gaps: no outbound channel integration, no pipeline timeout enforcement, duplicate Outcome enum, OCP violations in participant/channel factories. Correlation engine is single-node only. Session crate is orphaned. Store trait is overloaded. PRD alignment has significant gaps in v0.1 scope.
inputs_consulted: All key src/lib.rs and core source files

mentions.relevance_note: Code quality findings overlap with OCP violation and factory pattern issues
Auth architecture in channels and admin crates
CorrelationEngine RwLock contention and Store default impl performance
